channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Visitors Weigh Costs and Benefits of Attending Calgary Stampede Amid High Expenses
item.description: The Calgary Stampede, a major annual event in Canada, is drawing visitors who are carefully balancing their spending against the high costs associated with attending. Despite the event’s popularity, m...

item.title: Mumbai faces severe monsoon crisis with multiple casualties and infrastructure disruptions
item.description: Mumbai and surrounding regions are grappling with a severe monsoon crisis that has resulted in at least 13 fatalities and widespread disruptions. Heavy rainfall triggered landslides on the Mumbai-Pune...

item.title: Bien Makes History at Afro Nation Portugal with Kenyan Celebrities in Attendance
item.description: Kenyan artiste Bien-Aimé Baraza made history by becoming the first Kenyan solo performer at Afro Nation Portugal, delivering a groundbreaking Afro-pop set that captivated a global audience. The event,...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Investigates Police Corruption and Criminal Networks
item.description: The article details the ongoing investigations by the Madlanga Commission into allegations of police corruption, collusion with criminal networks, and systemic misconduct within South African law enfo...

item.title: Carlos Queiroz's Farewell: Lessons and Legacy for Ghanaian Football
item.description: Carlos Queiroz, the former Ghana national football team coach, delivered an emotional farewell message to Ghanaians, reflecting on his tenure and the journey of the Black Stars. In a heartfelt address...

item.title: Pakistan's President Zardari Embarks on Historic 4-Day Official Visit to Kyrgyzstan
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari is set to embark on a significant 4-day official visit to Kyrgyzstan, marking the first such visit by a Pakistani president in 21 years. This diplomatic mission, announced b...

item.title: Country Roads as Unofficial US Anthem at World Cup
item.description: The article discusses the unexpected popularity of the song 'Country Roads' as an unofficial anthem for the United States during the World Cup. Despite not being an official national anthem, the track...

item.title: Kilombero wetlands face environmental crisis due to farming and logging
item.description: Residents of Lumemo Ward in Ifakara Town Council are realizing the devastating impact of unregulated farming, brick-making, tree cutting, and charcoal burning on the Kilombero wetlands. These activiti...

item.title: Idaho mother indicted for murder of twin toddlers following vaccine-related claims
item.description: Andrea Shaw, 23, has been indicted on two counts of first-degree murder for the deaths of her twin toddlers, Dallas and Tyson, who were found dead in a shared bed on May 1, 2025. The Payette Police De...

item.title: DOE forecasts diesel and kerosene price increases in July
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) has forecasted that diesel prices may increase by P1.57 to P3.57 per liter, while kerosene prices could rise by P1.70 to P3.70 per liter. These adjustments are expected ...

item.title: England and Norway advance to World Cup quarter-finals after thrilling victories
item.description: England secured a dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico in the World Cup 2026 last-16 match in Mexico City, with Jude Bellingham scoring two goals in the first half. A red card for Jarell Quansah and a pen...

item.title: China tests nuclear-capable missile in South Pacific; Australia condemns move amid regional security tensions
item.description: China has announced plans to test-fire a nuclear-capable intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) in the South Pacific within 24 hours, according to diplomatic sources. This follows Australia's recen...

item.title: Canadian rescue team saves child amid Venezuela earthquake devastation
item.description: A Canadian urban search and rescue team from Burnaby played a critical role in locating a young survivor trapped in earthquake rubble in Venezuela, where over 3,342 people have died and 17,345 are hom...

item.title: Olivia Dean's Multiracial Heritage and Cultural Influences
item.description: Olivia Dean, a British singer of mixed ethnicity, has a unique cultural identity shaped by her English father and Jamaican-Guyanese mother. Born in London, she grew up in Highams Park, influenced by b...

item.title: Japan Funds New School Infrastructure in Tanzania
item.description: The Japanese government has invested over 269.3 million Tanzanian shillings to upgrade Orng’adida Primary School in Babati District, Manyara Region. The project, funded through Japan’s KUSANONE progra...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Election 2026: Karur Stampede Still Haunts Voters in Velusamypuram
item.description: The article discusses the lingering impact of a stampede incident that occurred during a political campaign in Tamil Nadu's Velusamypuram region. The incident, which happened during an election event,...

item.title: Gauteng Government Provides Trauma Counseling to Families of Ratanda Protest Victims
item.description: The Gauteng government has initiated trauma counseling for the families of two men killed during violent service delivery protests in Ratanda, following prolonged water outages that sparked community ...

item.title: House Prosecution Panel to Address Sara Duterte's Alleged Threats Against Marcos
item.description: The House prosecution panel has announced it will present evidence regarding Vice President Sara Duterte's alleged threats against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and his family, emphasizing that 'impu...

item.title: Switzerland's Energy Transition Doesn't Require New Nuclear Plants
item.description: A report by 19 energy specialists from ETH Zurich and the Paul Scherrer Institute concludes that Switzerland can achieve a secure energy transition through hydropower, solar energy, wind power, and el...

item.title: Tanzania Advances Nuclear Energy Plans with IAEA Support
item.description: Tanzania is making significant strides in its preparations to adopt nuclear energy for electricity generation, following the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) roadmap. The country is required ...
